title: CleanupBranchesResult
description: Output of `bin/cleanup-branches.sh`. The script first runs the same merged-into-base detection that `bin/check-stale-branches.sh` does, then either previews the list (default — `--yes` was not passed) or actually deletes the branches via `git branch -d` (safe deletion; refuses if a candidate isn't reachable from base). Three modes: `preview` (no --yes, no --dry-run; the JSON shows the list and the user must re-run with --yes to apply), `dry-run` (--dry-run; same shape, no apply), `applied` (--yes; deletions executed).

target.description: Repo path the cleanup ran against.

base_branch.description: Branch the merged-into-base check was run against.

mode.description: preview = invoked with neither --yes nor --dry-run (the JSON is shown to the human; nothing was deleted). dry-run = explicit --dry-run (no deletions). applied = --yes was passed (deletions executed).

candidates.description: Branches whose tip is reachable from the base branch — safe to delete with `git branch -d`. Mirrors the merged_into_base[] from the underlying StaleBranchesReport.

deleted.description: Branches actually deleted. Empty for preview / dry-run modes; equals candidates minus errors for applied mode.

errors.description: Branches the apply path tried to delete but failed (e.g. underlying git refused). Each entry includes the reason. Empty in non-apply modes.
